text: A new report shows a patient's cancer has recurred. Your supervisor is unaware. Most appropriate next step?
options: Inform your supervisor and prepare together.
Ask the supervisor to tell the patient.
Wait for the supervisor to find out.
rationale: Ethics: Physicians must share critical info with supervisors before delivery [cite: 12-21].

text: Which factor most increases a patient's risk of dying by suicide?
rationale: Dx: Previous attempts are the strongest predictor of completed suicide [cite: 22-28].

text: A patient on antipsychotics has fever (39C) and muscle rigidity. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: NMS. [cite_start]Elevated CK and rigidity are classic markers [cite: 29-37].

text: A fetus has spina bifida. Which maternal medication likely contributed?
rationale: Risk: Valproate is associated with neural tube defects [cite: 168-172].

text: A 65-year-old with microcytic anemia. Best investigation?
rationale: Dx: Iron deficiency in elderly is colon cancer until proven otherwise [cite: 173-177].
